posts.text:
There’s now a pattern of Israel targeting key actors involved in the repression of the Iranian people:

-  SNSC Secretary Ali Larijani (for his role in the January massacre) 
- Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani 
- Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib
Israel’s defense minister said that Israel killed Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib.  wsj.com/livecoverage/iran-us…

“As the world debates if or how the war should proceed, one voice is effectively missing: the voice of the Iranian people.”


NetBlocks (@netblocks)

⚠️ Update: The internet blackout in #Iran has entered its 19th day with international connectivity still unavailable after 432 hours to all but a chosen few.

As the world debates if or how the war should proceed, one voice is effectively missing: the voice of the Iranian people.





— https://nitter.net/netblocks/status/2034167457066135836#m

Iranians celebrate Chaharshanbeh Soori (festival of fire) in Tehran tonight


Vahid Online (@Vahid)

جشن #چهارشنبه‌سوری در #تهران
ویدیوهای دریافتی: 'سه‌شنبه ۲۶ اسفند'
#Iran #Tehran

Video
  



Video
  





— https://nitter.net/Vahid/status/2033977197040423356#m

Worth noting that Israel and the US haven't (yet) targeted the heads of the Islamic Republic's three branches of power:

- President Masoud Pezeshkian
- Judiciary Chief Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i
- Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf

The Islamic Republic is so threatened by a single citizen journalist in the West whose work has become integral to visually documenting what's happening inside Iran that it has resorted to doxxing him.
